board.title: Early Internet
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: The Internet has become ubiquitous in modern life. This puzzle traces key technologies and milestones that shaped its development, from early research networks in the late 1960s to the emergence of the World Wide Web in the mid-1990s.

clues.question: Distributed naming system introduced in 1983 that translates domain names into IP addresses
Wired networking technology invented at Xerox PARC by Robert Metcalfe for local area networks
Application-layer protocol used by browsers to request hypertext documents from web servers
Computer scientist who invented the World Wide Web at CERN
Internet protocol suite defining how data is packetized, addressed, transmitted, and routed; ultimately defeated the OSI model in the Protocol Wars
First graphical web browser to display images inline with text; developed at UIUC
Early distributed discussion system where users posted articles to topic-based newsgroups, predating the World Wide Web
Research laboratory where the World Wide Web was invented to streamline communication between researchers
1971 ARPANET messaging system introduced by Ray Tomlinson using the "@" symbol in addresses
Markup language used to semantically structure hypertext documents
Company behind the early web browser Navigator and creator of JavaScript; later lost the browser wars to Internet Explorer
Early packet-switching network funded by DARPA and precursor to the modern Internet
Standardized address used to identify resources on the Internet, invented by Tim Berners-Lee
